dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - May 12, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**MLB Recap: Guardians Outlast Angels, Yankees Cruise Past Orioles, and More**

In a busy day of MLB action, the Cleveland Guardians edged the Los Angeles Angels 3-2 behind a strong outing from Slade Cecconi, who struck out seven over four innings. Angel Martínez led the Angels with a pair of hits, including a home run. Vaughn Grissom's two-homer effort paced the Guardians.

Meanwhile, the New York Yankees rolled past the Baltimore Orioles 6-2, with Will Warren earning the win after five and a third scoreless innings. Trent Grisham and Paul Goldschmidt each homered for the Yankees.

In other results, the Washington Nationals routed the Cincinnati Reds 10-4 behind a strong team effort. Daylen Lile hit two home runs and drove in four for the Nationals. Paul Skenes threw a complete-game shutout for the Pittsburgh Pirates in their 3-1 win over the Colorado Rockies.

The Philadelphia Phillies took a 2-1 lead into the eighth inning and held on to beat the Boston Red Sox. Kyle Schwarber homered for the Phillies. The Tampa Bay Rays edged the Toronto Blue Jays 7-6 behind a strong outing from Shane McClanahan.

The New York Mets dominated the Detroit Tigers 10-2, with Freddy Peralta earning the win after six innings. Mike Yastrzemski homered for the Atlanta Braves in their 5-2 win over the Chicago Cubs.

The Chicago White Sox edged the Kansas City Royals 6-5 in extra innings. Chase Meidroth homered for the White Sox. Ryan Jeffers homered for the Minnesota Twins in their 3-0 shutout of the Miami Marlins.

The Milwaukee Brewers outlasted the San Diego Padres 6-4. Miguel Andujar homered for the Padres. The Texas Rangers took a 7-4 lead into the ninth inning and held on to beat the Arizona Diamondbacks. Ildemaro Vargas homered for the Diamondbacks.

The Seattle Mariners routed the Houston Astros 10-2 behind a strong outing from Bryan Woo. Randy Arozarena homered for the Mariners. The St. Louis Cardinals edged the Athletics 6-4 behind a strong team effort. Shea Langeliers homered for the Athletics.

Finally, the San Francisco Giants took a 2-0 lead into the fifth inning and held on to beat the Los Angeles Dodgers. Eric Haase homered for the Giants.
